title: Floating Table Runner Riser
summary: A tiny 1x8 riser on a recessed 1x2 frame — the frame sits well inside the top's edges, so the whole thing reads as floating rather than resting flat on the table.
description:
The entire visual trick is in how the base frame is positioned: instead of running the frame flush to the edges of the top board, it's set well inside the perimeter, so from eye level the top appears to float above the table with no visible support underneath it. It's a genuinely simple build — one small frame, one top board — but the placement of that frame is the one thing worth measuring carefully rather than eyeballing.

Glue the frame to the underside first and let it fully set before flipping the assembly over to drive nails through the top — nailing into a freshly glued joint before it's cured risks knocking it out of alignment.

steps.title: Build the base frame
steps.body: Build the base frame from the 1x2 pieces with 1-1/4" brad nails and wood glue.

steps.title: Attach the frame to the top
steps.body:
Apply glue to the frame and set it on the underside of the 1x8 top, positioned well inside the top's edges so the frame won't be visible from eye level — this inset is what makes the finished piece look like it's floating. Let the glue dry fully.

Once dry, flip the assembly over and attach with 1-1/4" brad nails and wood glue for a permanent hold.

images.alt: Floating table runner riser with a recessed base frame
